title: desmoncus horridus subsp. occidentalis
intro: Desmoncus horridus subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Western Horsetail Palm, is a type of climbing palm native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Desmoncus horridus subsp. occidentalis include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing them with other medicinal plants to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
